---
title: Reach Up
author: Fitz Koch
date: today
description:  Sources and logic for calculating Vermont's Reach Up program.
---



# Sources 
- See [here](https://dcf.vermont.gov/esd/laws-rules/proposed/P2200) for all the rules
- See  [Vermont Legislature Overview](https://legislature.vermont.gov/assets/Legislative-Reports/Reach-Up-Ratable-Reduction-Mar-2024.pdf) for easier to parse numbers.



# Overview
Per  [Vermont Legislature Overview](https://legislature.vermont.gov/assets/Legislative-Reports/Reach-Up-Ratable-Reduction-Mar-2024.pdf), basically, reach up is:

Basic Needs + Shelter Allowance + Special Housing Allowance (if applicable) = Total needs 

Then 

Total Needs x Ratable Percentage = Maximum Grant for Family size 

Then

Maximum Grant - Countable Income = Reach up Grant


# Logic 
1. Get the needs and shelter from the household size and location (in or out of chittenden)
2. Adjust the shelter with the special allowance if rent is over allowance (up to max of 90)
    * ignore subsidized housing in this
3. 

## Caveats
- This logic *ignores
